engineGuide.h2: Jeep Commander Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Jeep Commander across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: Mercedes-sourced CRD V6 brought torque and economy to UK Commander, but EGR complexity drove replacement demand.
compatibleVariants: Sport CRD, Limited CRD, Predator CRD, 4x4 CRD
commonFailures: EGR valve clogging - carbon buildup causes limp mode and rough idle after 70k miles
Turbo oil feed restriction - leads to turbo seizure on neglected high-mileage units
Injector wear - degrades fuel economy and causes misfires on older examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM642 Jeep Commander engine replacement

entries.title: 3.7L Petrol
entries.history: Entry-level PowerTech V6 offered adequate performance for base Sport trim, but timing chain issues affected longevity.
commonFailures: Exhaust manifold bolt failure - sheared bolts cause exhaust leak and ticking noise
Timing chain tensioner wear - plastic guides degrade causing rattle after 90k miles
Oil pump screen clogging - sludge reduces oil pressure on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for EKG Jeep Commander engine replacement

entries.title: 5.7L Petrol
entries.history: Premium Hemi V8 delivered flagship performance for Limited and Overland trims, but MDS lifter failures drove specialist rebuild demand.
compatibleVariants: Limited Hemi, Overland, 4x4 Hemi, Towing Spec
commonFailures: Hemi lifter collapse - MDS system lifters fail causing tappet noise and rough running
Camshaft wear - premature lobe wear on MDS-equipped engines after 80k miles
Oil consumption - piston ring wear increases oil burn on high-mileage Hemi units
entries.cta: Get quotes for EVL Jeep Commander engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Jeep Commander Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Jeep Commander's engine range includes the Mercedes-sourced 3.0L CRD diesel and Chrysler PowerTech/Hemi petrol units. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the problem groups below as a decision tool to compare repair costs versus replacement options for your specific Commander variant.

problems.h4: 3.0 CRD EGR Valve Failure - Why Jeep Commander Diesel Engines Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Commander Sport CRD, Limited CRD, Predator (2006-2010, OM642/OM642.980)
problems.rootCause: Carbon buildup from recirculated exhaust gases clogs the EGR valve and intake manifold, restricting airflow and triggering limp mode.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - EGR Cleaning - Remove and clean valve and passages
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: EGR valve removal, ultrasonic cleaning, intake manifold decarbonising
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; may recur within 12-18 months if driving style unchanged

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - EGR Replacement - Fit new or reconditioned valve
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New EGR valve fitted with updated gaskets and software calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Reliable solution if intake system also cleaned; 30k+ mile expectation

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt OM642 Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt OM642 with upgraded EGR components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage Commanders; addresses all wear items simultaneously

problems.recommendation: If your Commander is under 80k miles and otherwise well-maintained, EGR cleaning or replacement offers good value. For Commanders over 100k miles with additional symptoms (turbo noise, injector rattle), a rebuilt OM642 engine often proves more cost-effective long-term.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt OM642 engine prices for your Jeep Commander

problems.h4: 4.7L PowerTech Timing Chain Failure - Catastrophic Risk for Jeep Commander V8
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Commander Sport 4.7L, Limited 4.7L, Rocky Mountain (2006-2009, PowerTech V8)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioners lose pressure and plastic guides fracture, allowing chain slack that causes timing jump and valve-to-piston contact.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Timing Chain Kit - Replace chains, guides, tensioners
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full timing chain system replacement with upgraded tensioners and guides
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine shows no valve damage; requires specialist assessment first

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt PowerTech 4.7L - Supply & fit rebuilt unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt PowerTech V8 with reinforced timing components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for Commanders with confirmed timing jump or valve contact

problems.recommendation: Timing chain failure on the 4.7L PowerTech is often catastrophic. If you hear rattling on cold start or have a check engine light for cam/crank correlation, get an immediate compression test. For confirmed damage, a rebuilt engine is typically more economical than attempting valve repair on an aged block.
problems.cta: Get quotes for PowerTech 4.7L Jeep Commander engine replacement

problems.h4: 5.7L Hemi MDS Lifter Collapse - Premium Engine, Premium Failure Risk
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Commander Limited Hemi, Overland (2006-2010, EVL Hemi 5.7L)
problems.rootCause: Multi-Displacement System (MDS) lifters collapse due to oil starvation or wear, causing tappet noise, camshaft damage, and loss of cylinder deactivation function.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Lifter & Cam Replacement - Replace failed lifters and damaged cam
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder heads, replace MDS lifters, inspect/replace camshaft, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if cam damage is minimal; requires precise torque specs and break-in

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Hemi 5.7L - Supply & fit rebuilt unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt Hemi with upgraded MDS components, non-MDS option available, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term solution for Overland/Limited Commanders; eliminates MDS complexity if desired

problems.recommendation: Hemi lifter failure is expensive to repair correctly. For Commanders valued under £8,000, a rebuilt Hemi with optional non-MDS conversion often delivers better reliability and value than attempting a partial repair.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt Hemi 5.7L engine prices for your Jeep Commander

problems.h4: OM642 Turbocharger Oil Feed Failure - Diesel Commander Performance Loss
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Commander Sport CRD, Limited CRD (2006-2010, OM642)
problems.rootCause: Restricted oil feed line or clogged turbo oil drain causes bearing starvation, leading to turbo seizure, loss of boost, and potential engine oil contamination.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Turbo Oil Line Service - Clean/replace feed and drain lines
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, clean/replace oil feed and drain lines, replace gaskets, refill oil
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative measure; only suitable before turbo damage occurs

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Turbo Replacement - Fit new or reconditioned turbocharger
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove failed turbo, fit reconditioned unit with new gaskets and oil lines
repairOptions.longevity: Reliable if engine oil is clean and no metal contamination occurred

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt OM642 Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt OM642 with upgraded turbo and oil system components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if turbo failure caused metal debris in engine oil or bearing damage

problems.recommendation: If your Commander shows turbo whine, blue smoke, or loss of power, get an oil analysis immediately. For confirmed turbo failure with clean oil, turbo replacement is cost-effective. If metal debris is present, a rebuilt engine protects your investment.
problems.cta: Find OM642 turbo and engine replacement options for your Jeep Commander

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Jeep Commander
finalCta.paragraph: The Jeep Commander's engine issues are well-documented, but replacement doesn't have to mean main-dealer prices. EngineMarket connects you with trusted UK specialists who supply rebuilt OM642, PowerTech and Hemi engines with upgraded components addressing the original failure points. Every unit includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery options. Compare quotes from multiple specialists in seconds and get your Commander back on the road without the premium markup.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Jeep Commander Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Jeep Commander and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Jeep Commander Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Jeep Commander is listed below with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Jeep Commander 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0L CRD diesel, sourced from Mercedes-Benz, was the dominant engine choice for UK Jeep Commander owners seeking torque and fuel economy. Fitted to Sport, Limited and Predator trims from 2006-2010, this OM642-family unit delivered strong low-end pulling power for towing but requires diligent EGR and turbo maintenance.
items.cta: Get quotes for Jeep Commander 3.0L diesel engines

items.title: Jeep Commander 3.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: The entry-level 3.7L PowerTech V6 powered the base Sport trim and special editions like the 65th Anniversary and Rocky Mountain. Adequate for daily driving but known for exhaust manifold bolt failures and timing chain tensioner wear on higher-mileage examples.
items.cta: Compare Jeep Commander 3.7L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jeep Commander 4.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: The mid-range 4.7L PowerTech V8 offered enhanced torque for towing and was available on Sport and Limited trims. Updated to 305hp for 2008-2009 models but retained complex timing chain architecture prone to failure without meticulous maintenance.
items.cta: Compare Jeep Commander 4.7L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jeep Commander 5.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: The flagship 5.7L Hemi V8 powered Limited and Overland trims, delivering premium performance with Multi-Displacement System (MDS) cylinder deactivation. MDS lifter collapse and camshaft wear are the dominant failure patterns driving replacement enquiries for these high-value variants.
items.cta: Compare Jeep Commander 5.7L petrol engine prices

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your Jeep Commander needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Jeep Commander specialists.

engineYears.h2: Jeep Commander Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Jeep Commander's UK production timeline (2006-2010), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Launch Year: PowerTech Petrol & OM642 Diesel Debut
years.description: The Commander launched with three petrol engines and the Mercedes-sourced OM642 diesel for UK/European markets.
keyChanges: First-year production introduced 3.7L EKG, 4.7L PowerTech V8, 5.7L Hemi and OM642 diesel
XH diesel export version built at Magna Steyr, Austria for UK market
Base/Sport and Limited trims offered with multiple engine options
mainEngines: OM642 - 3.0L diesel (Sport CRD, Limited CRD, Predator)
knownFor: First-year sensor and electrical gremlins on early VINs
OM642 diesel immediately popular for UK towing and economy
PowerTech V8 timing chain concerns emerge in owner forums
65th Anniversary Edition launched with unique badging and equipment
years.cta: Get quotes for 2006 Jeep Commander replacement engines
years.ticker: Jeep Commander 2006 engine replacement • OM642 diesel EGR failure • PowerTech 4.7L timing chain rattle

years.preview: Mid-Cycle Update: Overland Trim & PowerTech Refresh
years.description: 2007 introduced the flagship Overland trim and updated PowerTech V8 output, while diesel demand grew in the UK.
keyChanges: Overland trim launched exclusively with 5.7L Hemi EVL engine
PowerTech 4.7L output increased to 305hp for 2008 model year (late 2007 production)
Rocky Mountain Edition introduced with 3.7L or 4.7L engine options
OM642 diesel refined for improved emissions compliance
knownFor: Overland Hemi MDS lifter failures begin appearing in high-mileage examples
OM642 EGR clogging reports increase as first diesel Commanders reach 70k miles
PowerTech 4.7L timing chain tensioner wear documented on early units
Rocky Mountain Edition gains cult following among UK off-road enthusiasts
years.cta: Get quotes for 2007 Jeep Commander replacement engines
years.ticker: Jeep Commander 2007 Hemi engine • OM642 turbo failure • PowerTech 4.7L rebuild cost

years.preview: Production Maturity: Refined OM642 & Hemi Update
years.description: Late-production Commanders featured refined engine calibrations and improved component durability across the range.
keyChanges: 5.7L Hemi output increased to 360hp for 2009-2010 model years
OM642 diesel received updated EGR and turbo components for improved reliability
PowerTech 4.7L production ended after 2009; Hemi and diesel continued
mainEngines: OM642 - 3.0L diesel refined spec (Sport CRD, Limited CRD)
knownFor: Late-production OM642 units show improved EGR durability
360hp Hemi variants command premium in replacement market
PowerTech 4.7L scarcity increases as units age and fail

years.preview: 2010 Commanders preferred for replacement engine sourcing
years.cta: Get quotes for 2008-2009 Jeep Commander replacement engines
years.ticker: Jeep Commander 2009 engine • Hemi 360hp replacement • OM642 late-spec diesel

years.preview: Final Production Year: Most Refined Specification
years.description: The final model year represented the most refined Commander specification, ideal for donor vehicles and replacement engine sourcing.
keyChanges: Final production year for Jeep Commander (XK/XH)
Only Sport, Limited and diesel variants continued; Overland discontinued
5.7L Hemi retained 360hp output; OM642 diesel at peak refinement
No new engine codes introduced; focus on component quality improvements
mainEngines: OM642 - 3.0L diesel refined spec (Sport CRD, Limited CRD)
knownFor: 2010 Commanders most sought-after for low-mileage replacement engines
OM642 units from 2010 show best long-term reliability in UK fleet data
Hemi 360hp variants retain strong enthusiast demand for performance rebuilds
Final-year production benefits from all prior running changes and fixes

faq.h2: Jeep Commander Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Jeep Commander engine replacement cost?
items.answer: Jeep Commander engine replacement costs range from £1,660 for a rebuilt 3.0L CRD diesel to £6,800 for a remanufactured 5.7L Hemi, depending on engine code, variant and condition. Get instant tailored quotes to compare prices from UK specialists.
keyPoints: OM642 diesel rebuilt: £1,660-£3,950 supply only
PowerTech petrol rebuilt: £1,805-£4,200 supply only
Hemi V8 rebuilt: £3,200-£6,800 supply only
Supply & fit adds £650-£1,450 labour typically
All prices include 12-month warranty minimum
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Jeep Commander engine prices for your variant

items.question: What engine codes fit my Jeep Commander?
items.answer: Your Jeep Commander may have OM642 (3.0L diesel), EKG (3.7L petrol), PowerTech 4.7L, or EVL (5.7L Hemi). Enter your registration or VIN to instantly verify the exact engine code compatible with your vehicle.
keyPoints: OM642/OM642.980/EXL for 3.0L CRD diesel variants
EKG code for 3.7L PowerTech V6 petrol Sport models
PowerTech 4.7L for mid-range V6/V8 petrol trims
EVL code for 5.7L Hemi V8 Limited and Overland
DVLA lookup confirms exact fitment instantly
items.cta: Find compatible engine codes for your Jeep Commander

items.question: How long does Jeep Commander engine replacement take?
items.answer: Most Jeep Commander supply & fit engine replacements are completed within 3-5 working days from quote acceptance, with next-day engine dispatch available on OM642 and common PowerTech units from UK stock.
keyPoints: Next-day dispatch on OM642 diesel from UK stock
2-3 days typical for PowerTech petrol engine sourcing
3-5 working days for complete supply & fit service
Recovery and old engine removal included in timeline
Post-fit testing and handover on completion day
items.cta: Get a timeline quote for your Jeep Commander engine fit

items.question: Is a rebuilt Jeep Commander engine reliable?
items.answer: Professionally rebuilt Jeep Commander engines include upgraded components addressing known failure points and carry a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty, often proving more reliable than high-mileage used units.
keyPoints: Rebuilt units address EGR, timing chain or MDS failure points
Bench or dyno tested before dispatch to UK customers
12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard across network
Upgraded oil feeds, tensioners or lifters improve longevity
Specialist rebuilders focus on Commander-specific failure patterns
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs used Jeep Commander engine options

items.question: Can I replace my Commander petrol engine with diesel?
items.answer: Fuel type conversion on the Jeep Commander requires DVLA notification, MoT retest, and extensive modifications to fuel system, ECU and exhaust - not recommended and rarely cost-effective versus like-for-like replacement.
keyPoints: Fuel conversion requires DVLA notification and re-registration
ECU, fuel pump, injectors and exhaust must be swapped
MoT emissions test must be passed post-conversion
Labour costs often exceed price difference between engine types
Like-for-like replacement preserves warranty and compliance
items.cta: Get quotes for like-for-like Jeep Commander engine replacement

items.question: What warranty comes with a Jeep Commander replacement engine?
items.answer: All engines supplied through EngineMarket carry a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour at approved UK fitting centres, with extended warranty options available on remanufactured units.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on rebuilt units
Warranty valid at approved UK fitting garages nationwide
Parts and labour covered for warranty-qualifying failures
Extended warranty options on remanufactured Hemi units
Warranty terms confirmed with chosen supplier pre-purchase
items.cta: Check warranty terms for your Jeep Commander engine quote

items.question: How do I know which Jeep Commander engine I need?
items.answer: Check your V5C registration document, locate the engine code stamp on the block, or enter your registration on our tool to instantly identify your exact Jeep Commander engine specification and compatible replacement options.
keyPoints: V5C document lists engine code and fuel type
Engine code stamped on block near cylinder head
DVLA registration lookup confirms exact variant and engine
OM642 for diesel, EKG/PowerTech/EVL for petrol variants
Our comparison tool matches code to available replacements
items.cta: Identify your Jeep Commander engine code instantly

items.question: Are Jeep Commander engines interchangeable between years?
items.answer: Most Jeep Commander engines interchange across 2006-2010 model years, but ECU mapping, sensor fitment and emissions components must be verified for exact compatibility before ordering a replacement unit.
keyPoints: OM642 diesel codes interchangeable with calibration check
PowerTech 4.7L 235hp and 305hp variants require ECU match
Hemi EVL 330hp and 360hp need MDS system verification
Sensor harness and mounting points consistent across years
Our specialists confirm compatibility before dispatch
items.cta: Verify cross-year compatibility for your Jeep Commander engine

items.question: What's included in a Jeep Commander supply and fit service?
items.answer: Supply and fit service includes engine delivery, removal of your old unit, professional installation by certified specialists, fluid refill, post-fit testing and vehicle handover - all coordinated through one quote.
keyPoints: Engine delivery to certified fitting garage included
Old engine removal and disposal handled by specialist
Professional installation with torque specs and calibration
Fluid refill, system bleed and road testing completed
Vehicle valeted and ready for collection post-completion
items.cta: Find Jeep Commander supply & fit specialists near you

items.question: Can I install a Jeep Commander engine myself?
items.answer: Professional fitting is recommended for Jeep Commander engine replacement due to complex ECU calibration and torque specifications, but we support independent garages with compatibility documentation and technical guidance.
keyPoints: ECU adaptation requires specialist diagnostic equipment
Torque specs and timing procedures critical for longevity
Warranty may require installation at approved centre
Compatibility documentation supplied for independent fitters
Technical support available throughout installation process
